theorem: For any plane map written in k[y][x] with max x-degree N>=2, the coefficient of x^{2N-1} in det JF is N times the Wronskian of the leading pair (P_N, Q_N), independent of all lower terms. Const Jac forces the leading pair to vanish or reduce to E_x (P_N=0, Q_N const in y). Induction on N yields deg_x(f)<=1; T4 classifies N=1 as tame.
plane_jc_status: Wronskian isolation identity SEALED (N<=5, dy<=2); leading-pair forcing verified in the zero-tower slice only — x-drop induction NOT closed. The I1 identity is checked for symbolic free coeffs of y-degree <= 2; the machine check is bounded by N<=5, dy<=2 (NOT degree-free). This does NOT by itself establish the plane JC; it seals one algebraic identity in the x-filtration reduction.
